glue.note: Reference benchmark for LTZGLUE comparative assessment. Auto-discovers all configs (12 total incl. ax, mnli_matched, mnli_mismatched).

blurb.note: Reference benchmark for DrBenchmark comparative assessment. Uses a custom loading script — DA step will be auto-skipped.

mathdial.note: Reference benchmark for MRBench comparative assessment. Single flat dataset (train/test).

mmlu.note: Reference benchmark for MILU and ArabicMMLU comparative assessments. Auto-discovers all configs (57 subjects + 'all' + 'auxiliary_train').

crisisbench.note: Reference benchmark for WXImpactBench comparative assessment. Consolidation of 8 crisis NLP datasets (CrisisNLP, CrisisLex, etc.). Fallback rule — WXImpactBench is the first in its niche; CrisisBench is the closest established standard for disaster text classification.

cnn_dailymail.note: Reference benchmark for CrisisLTLSum comparative assessment. Most-cited English-language summarization benchmark (162/226 publications). Fallback rule — CrisisLTLSum is the first crisis timeline summarization benchmark; CNN/DailyMail is the established standard for abstractive summarization.

asap_pp.note: Reference benchmark for LAILA comparative assessment. Leading English-language AES dataset (Mathias & Bhattacharyya, LREC 2018). Not on HuggingFace — original data on Kaggle (asap-aes).

civil_comments.note: Reference benchmark for ToxiGen comparative assessment. Jigsaw Unintended Bias dataset (Borkan et al., WWW 2019). ~2M news comments with toxicity scores and identity group annotations. Fallback rule — ToxiGen compares against many datasets without singling one out; Civil Comments is the most widely used English toxicity classification benchmark with identity-bias annotations.

flores200.note: Reference benchmark for AFRIDOC-MT comparative assessment. Standard multilingual MT evaluation benchmark (200 languages, sentence-level). NLLB Team et al. 2022 'No Language Left Behind'. Primary rule — AFRIDOC-MT explicitly positions itself against FLORES-200 in Table 1 as filling the document-level MT gap for African languages.

massive.note: Reference benchmark for INJONGO comparative assessment. Largest multilingual intent detection and slot-filling dataset (51 languages, 60 intents, 55 slots). FitzGerald et al., ACL 2023. Primary rule — INJONGO explicitly positions itself against MASSIVE's limited African language coverage (3 languages) and Western-centric cultural bias.

swda.note: Reference benchmark for HOPE comparative assessment. Standard dialogue act classification benchmark (Godfrey et al. 1992). Primary rule — HOPE explicitly positions itself against Switchboard, evaluating SPARTA on both.

samsum.note: Reference benchmark for MentalClouds comparative assessment. Most-cited English dialogue summarization benchmark (~740 citations, Gliwa et al. EMNLP 2019). Fallback rule — MentalClouds extends MEMO without positioning against a single external benchmark; SAMSum is the standard for abstractive dialogue summarization.
